content.plain_english: This week your child is meeting the words for time — before, after, yesterday, tomorrow — and starting to read a clock to o'clock and half past.
content.in_school: Children put events from their day in order — wake up, breakfast, school, dinner, bed. They learn the names of the days of the week and the months of the year. They look at a real clock and say what time it is when both hands point to a number they recognise.
content.home_activity: Point to a kitchen clock four times across one weekend day — once at o'clock, once at half past, twice more whenever feels natural. Ask your child what time it is. End the day asking which days of the week we've had so far.
dinner_table_questions: What did you do this morning? And before that?
What time is it on the kitchen clock right now?

key_vocabulary.definition: When the minute hand points to 12 and the hour hand to a number.
When the minute hand points to 6.
Three different lengths of time we measure life with.

example_questions: What time is shown when both clock hands point to 12?
Name the months of the year in order.
content.times_table_tip: Year 1 children practise counting in 2s, 5s and 10s. There are 12 numbers on a clock face — count them in 2s: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12.
